contract_comparison.reason: SGLang can use tl.dot over BLOCK_H x BLOCK_N because selected K/V are head-shared. TorchTitan currently has per-head selected K/V, so a literal BLOCK_H dot would compute cross-head scores unless the ABI changes or the schedule is reimplemented for per-head K/V. Training also needs backward scatter-add into q/k/v/compressed_k/compressed_v, which the inference kernel does not provide.
contract_comparison.sglang_selected_kv_layout: [tokens, topk, dim], effectively shared across query heads
contract_comparison.torchtitan_training_layout: [batch, seq, heads, dim] plus [batch, groups, heads, dim]

memory_estimates.note: The final training backend must not save or globally materialize the per-head selected K/V state at S=4096. Temporary per-tile register/shared-memory staging is fine; full selected-state packing is not.

mfma_reuse_estimate.read: For per-head K/V, an SGLang-style Q[BLOCK_H,D] x K[BLOCK_H*BLOCK_N,D]^T dot computes BLOCK_H times more score entries than needed for that head tile. This is why the useful reuse is the schedule pattern, not the literal inference ABI.

recommended_bridge.action: Port the SGLang online-softmax/MFMA schedule ideas, but keep TorchTitan's training ABI and per-head selected K/V semantics.
Keep manual_stream-style saved row_max, row_sum, and output; do not save selected K/V or probabilities.
Add a matching backward recompute kernel that emits dQ plus selected dK/dV and scatter-adds into local and compressed states.
A temporary packed/head-shared SGLang-style forward probe is useful only if it is clearly labeled non-production and not used for S4096 training promotion.
